news.title: Apple AI Lawsuit Settlement Puts Spotlight On iPhone Marketing And Risk
news.summary: Apple, ticker NasdaqGS:AAPL, has agreed to a $250 million settlement in a class-action lawsuit. The case centers on claims that 2024 iPhone models misrepresented artificial intelligence features to consumers. The settlement is subject to court approval and could result in payments to eligible customers. For a company whose flagship iPhone line underpins both hardware sales and services revenue, any legal dispute tied directly to product marketing tends to draw close investor attention. The...

news.title: Brunswick and Callaway Golf Company Shares Are Falling, What You Need To Know
news.summary: A number of stocks fell in the morning session after consumer discretionary stocks pulled back, led by a plunge in Lululemon as the company cut its full-year revenue guidance to $11.0–$11.15 billion from $11.35–$11.5 billion, citing weaker US consumer traffic, brand backlash on social media, and underperforming product launches.

news.title: Gold Stocks Are in a Bear Market. The Case for Buying.
news.summary: Gold stocks are way down, and anyone seeking a bargain should at least give them a look.  The VanEck Gold Miners exchange-traded fund—home to some of the largest miners, such as  Newmont  and  Barrick Mining  —has slid 24% from its record high hit in late February.  The stocks are so-called leveraged plays on the commodity—when the price of the commodity falls, the stocks fall harder.

news.title: Why Vanguard’s VOO Won the Trillion-Dollars-in-Assets Race
news.summary: Vanguard’s S&P 500 exchange-traded fund—so popular it’s known simply by its ticker symbol VOO—just topped $1 trillion in assets, the first ETF to mark that milestone.  VOO, which launched in 2010, got there before two rivals with head starts:  BlackRock  ETF, which began trading in 2000, has $859 billion in assets and the  ETF, the first U.S. ETF to debut in 1993, has $787 billion, according to ETF Database.  VOO charges an expense ratio of 0.03% versus 0.09% for  State Street  ; the iShares ETF also charges 0.03%.
